channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. to reinstate Hormuz blockade. And, states sue over Paramount-Warner deal
item.description: The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.
item.content:encoded:
The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.

(Image credit: -)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'The Trojan Teddy Bear': The promise and peril of childhood in the age of AI
item.description: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.content:encoded: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: The U.S. is set to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military announced it will begin its blockade of Iranian ships over the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, as Iran vowed to assert its own control over the critical international waterway.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military announced it will begin its blockade of Iranian ships over the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, as Iran vowed to assert its own control over the critical international waterway.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says the U.S. will collect tolls and impose blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, man killed by federal agents in Maine, states sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
item.content:encoded: Trump says the U.S. will collect tolls and impose blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, man killed by federal agents in Maine, states sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Platner's voters are reeling as Maine Democratic Party races to choose his replacement
item.description: Graham Platner generated enthusiasm in Maine with his progressive policies. Now he's out of the race, and his supporters have to decide what's next, as Maine Democrats prepare to choose a nominee.
item.content:encoded:
Graham Platner generated enthusiasm in Maine with his progressive policies. Now he's out of the race, and his supporters have to decide what's next, as Maine Democrats prepare to choose a nominee.

(Image credit: Tamara Keith/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Some states will ask voters to make it harder to pass constitutional amendments
item.description: Several states have ballot measures this year that could raise the thresholds needed to pass state constitutional amendments. Many advocates are critical of such limits on direct democracy.
item.content:encoded:
Several states have ballot measures this year that could raise the thresholds needed to pass state constitutional amendments. Many advocates are critical of such limits on direct democracy.

(Image credit: Kyle Rivas)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: Trump's National Guard deployment in D.C. has been extended until 2029
item.description: President Trump's deployment of the National Guard to Washington, D.C., has been extended several times. Now it's set to last until Inauguration Day 2029.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's deployment of the National Guard to Washington, D.C., has been extended several times. Now it's set to last until Inauguration Day 2029.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Your cheat sheet to the teams headed to the World Cup semi-finals
item.description: We're at the final stretch of the World Cup. It feels like just yesterday, international fans were arriving in the U.S. and marveling at Buc-ee's and unlimited free soda.
item.content:encoded:
We're at the final stretch of the World Cup. It feels like just yesterday, international fans were arriving in the U.S. and marveling at Buc-ee's and unlimited free soda.

(Image credit: Justin Setterfield)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Death toll from a Bangkok music bar fire rises to 30, dozens remain in hospital
item.description: More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.
item.content:encoded:
More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The star-studded World Cup semifinals kick off with France vs. Spain
item.description: The expanded World Cup allowed FIFA to introduce a new seeding system that placed the world's top teams in opposite corners of the knockout bracket. Now, the semifinals are both heavyweight matchups.
item.content:encoded:
The expanded World Cup allowed FIFA to introduce a new seeding system that placed the world's top teams in opposite corners of the knockout bracket. Now, the semifinals are both heavyweight matchups.

(Image credit: Florencia Tan Jun, Molly Darlington, Richard Pelham, David Ramos)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan
